ir_specs.statement: The `who invocation` -q option prints only the login names and the number of users logged on.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Print only the login names and the number of users logged on.

ir_specs.statement: The `who invocation` -r option prints the current (and maybe previous) run-level of the init process.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Print the current (and maybe previous) run-level of the init process.

ir_specs.statement: The `who invocation` -s option ignored; for compatibility with other versions of who .
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Ignored; for compatibility with other versions of who .

ir_specs.statement: The `who invocation` -t option prints last system clock change.
The `who invocation` -u option after the login time, print the number of hours and minutes that the user has been idle.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: After the login time, print the number of hours and minutes that the user has been idle.

ir_specs.statement: The `who invocation` -w option after each login name print a character indicating the user’s message status:.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: After each login name print a character indicating the user’s message status:.

ir_specs.statement: who prints information about users who are currently logged on.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: who prints information about users who are currently logged on.

ir_specs.statement: If given no non-option arguments, who prints the following information for each user currently logged on: login name, terminal line, login time, and remote hostname or X display.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: If given no non-option arguments, who prints the following information for each user currently logged on: login name, terminal line, login time, and remote hostname or X display.

ir_specs.statement: If given one non-option argument, who uses that instead of a default system-maintained file (often /var/run/utmp or /etc/utmp ) as the name of the file containing the record of users logged on.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: If given one non-option argument, who uses that instead of a default system-maintained file (often /var/run/utmp or /etc/utmp ) as the name of the file containing the record of users logged on.

ir_specs.statement: /var/log/wtmp is commonly given as an argument to who to look at who has previously logged on.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: /var/log/wtmp is commonly given as an argument to who to look at who has previously logged on.

ir_specs.statement: If given two non-option arguments, who prints only the entry for the user running it (determined from its standard input), preceded by the hostname.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: If given two non-option arguments, who prints only the entry for the user running it (determined from its standard input), preceded by the hostname.

ir_specs.statement: Traditionally, the two arguments given are ‘ am i ’, as in ‘ who am i ’.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Traditionally, the two arguments given are ‘ am i ’, as in ‘ who am i ’.

ir_specs.statement: Timestamps are listed according to the time zone rules specified by the TZ environment variable, or by the system default rules if TZ is not set.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Timestamps are listed according to the time zone rules specified by the TZ environment variable, or by the system default rules if TZ is not set.

ir_specs.statement: See Specifying the Time Zone with TZ in The GNU C Library Reference Manual .
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: See Specifying the Time Zone with TZ in The GNU C Library Reference Manual .

ir_specs.statement: The program accepts the following options.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: The program accepts the following options.
